findings.summary: Runtime query readiness is strongly gated through artifact metadata, checkpoints, run records, and validateBookArtifactSet, but the TS runtime gate itself still checks graphrag/runs entries mainly for file presence and does not fully compare run-record artifactIds, inputFingerprint, tool/schema version, and generation time against metadata rows.

Capability projection now binds package artifacts to bookId, producerRunId, stage/provider fingerprints, corpus content hash, and book-scoped output paths, but the manifest-first runtime gate does not fully enforce the declared bidirectional binding among producerRunIds, graphrag/runs records, artifact metadata rows, and files closure.

Runtime compatibility artifacts include package/runtime metadata and four semantic schema digests, but the enforced TS gate still compares only outputManifestSchemaDigest, parquetSchemaDigest, lancedbSchemaDigest, and artifactMetadataSchemaDigest; embedding model/dimension, package layout, and runtime reader compatibility are not independently fail-closed.

The previously failing manifest-first catalog test now passes, and runtime/privacy/route tests pass, but executable coverage still lacks negative cases for malformed run-record semantic binding and broader runtime compatibility fields such as embedding dimension and package layout/runtime reader mismatch.
